description_long: This object is a large, dome-shaped stone with a smooth, rounded surface and a uniform brownish-gray coloration. Its form is nearly hemispherical, with a broad, gently curved top and a slightly flattened base, suggesting stability when placed on the ground. The surface appears mostly featureless, with only minor irregularities and subtle shading that hint at a natural stone texture. There are a few small, darker triangular marks near the base, possibly representing chips or natural inclusions. The stone's size and shape make it suitable as an obstacle or barrier in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or plazas. Its solid, monolithic appearance suggests significant weight and durability.
description: A large, dome-shaped, smooth brownish-gray stone with a rounded top and a few small dark marks near the base. Its solid, monolithic form makes it suitable as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: The stone appears as a smooth, rounded dome with a uniform brownish-gray color and no visible markings.
description_view_1: This view reveals two small, dark triangular marks near the lower right edge of the stone, adding minor surface detail.
description_view_2: Three small, dark triangular marks are visible near the base, providing the most surface detail of all views.
description_view_3: The stone is mostly smooth and rounded, with only a single small dark mark visible on the left edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Mostly featureless with a few small, irregular dark marks near the base; no regular pattern or artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and slightly matte, suggesting moderate weathering but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically placed as a visual and physical barrier, contrasting with paved surfaces but blending with other natural stone elements in landscaped urban areas.
